steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for a drawback of Hidalgo and colleagues' method according to the text. The passage states the limitation directly: "data from Places API tend to be restricted to places that are customer facing." That is the only drawback mentioned.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It depends upon data that are likely to be outdated.': The passage says nothing about the data being outdated. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It is based on recent advancements from other fields that have yet to be applied outside of those contexts.': The passage makes no mention of borrowing from other fields or of limited applicability. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It may lead to conclusions that are not reflective of all the amenities in a given neighborhood.': If the data only capture customer-facing places, non-customer-facing amenities are excluded, so conclusions about neighborhood amenities would be incomplete. This matches the stated limitation exactly. ✓
  • Choice D — 'It is likely to contribute to inaccurate identifications of the boundaries of specific neighborhoods.': The passage discusses the mix of businesses within neighborhoods, not how neighborhood boundaries are drawn. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
